steps.title: Identify the antecedent
steps.content: The blank is a possessive modifying "compelling narrative." The thing that owns the narrative is The Poem of the Cid — a single poem (singular). So we need a singular possessive pronoun, not a plural one and not a contraction.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: pronoun-antecedent agreement. The antecedent is "The Poem of the Cid" — singular. Rule: a possessive pronoun must match its antecedent in number, and a possessive (not a contraction) is needed before a noun.
  • Choice A — "they're": Contraction of "they are" — not a possessive and plural. Both errors. ✗
  • Choice B — "its": Singular possessive pronoun. Correctly matches the singular antecedent "The Poem of the Cid." ✓
  • Choice C — "their": Plural possessive — wrong number for a single poem. ✗
  • Choice D — "it's": Contraction of "it is" — not a possessive; reads as "it is compelling narrative," which is ungrammatical. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
